meta.scene_summary: We're busy teaching AI to act like seniors while we spend our days babysitting the hallucinations and cleaning up the data disasters.

cooking.headline: Nvidia is basically funding its own revenue growth via a GPU money-laundering loop
cooking.summary: Nvidia's customers are buying GPUs using money backed by investors who are betting on Nvidia's growth. It's the corporate equivalent of paying your credit card bill with a cash advance from the same bank.
cooking.plain_english: Nvidia, CoreWeave, and Nebius are involved in a circular financing arrangement where investment capital is used to purchase GPUs, effectively inflating revenue figures.

cooking.headline: Grok's CLI tool is essentially a 'send my secrets to Elon' button
cooking.summary: Developers discovered that Grok's build CLI sends a surprising amount of local context back to xAI servers under the hood. It turns out your 'local' AI coding assistant is actually just a pen pal with a very curious roommate in the cloud.
cooking.plain_english: The command-line interface tool for xAI's Grok transmits more local developer data to the company's servers than many users might expect.

cooking.headline: Terry Tao is basically giving AI a promotion to senior dev
cooking.summary: Fields Medalist Terry Tao is exploring how coding agents can bridge the gap between legacy apps and modern features. It's like watching a genius try to teach a group project partner how to actually finish the assignment without breaking the repository.
cooking.plain_english: Mathematician Terry Tao explores the potential for artificial intelligence coding agents to assist in updating and maintaining software applications.

cooking.headline: Distributing AI across your friend's laptop is the new peer-to-peer file sharing
cooking.summary: Iroh is letting you run LLMs across a mesh network, essentially turning every device you own into a tiny, struggling intern. It's a great way to use your phone's battery to help your laptop hallucinate faster.
cooking.plain_english: This project allows multiple devices to work together to run artificial intelligence models across a decentralized network.

cooking.headline: ClickHouse just made PgBouncer sweat by scaling it to 4x throughput
cooking.summary: The team essentially gave PgBouncer a triple-shot of espresso to handle massive database connections without falling over. It's the infrastructure equivalent of upgrading from a single-lane road to a highway just in time for rush hour.
cooking.plain_english: ClickHouse engineers optimized the PgBouncer database connection pooler to support four times the amount of traffic compared to its previous configuration.

cooking.headline: SQLite is finally letting you enforce types, so stop putting 'banana' in your integer columns
cooking.summary: SQLite built its brand on being the database that never yells at you for bad data types. Now that strict mode is here, it turns out the freedom to store anything anywhere was just a long-term prank on our future selves.
cooking.plain_english: SQLite has introduced a feature that requires columns to strictly adhere to specific data types, preventing developers from accidentally storing incorrect information.

cooked.headline: Big Tech is now officially out-polluting entire European nations
cooked.summary: Building a digital brain that can write poetry is impressive, but it apparently requires the carbon footprint of a small country to keep the lights on. We're essentially trading the ozone layer for the ability to ask AI to explain why we're running out of electricity.
cooked.plain_english: The carbon emissions from data centers used by major technology companies have increased significantly, reaching a combined level comparable to one-third of the total emissions of France.

cooked.headline: Windows 11 has a 'do not disturb' button for everything except the tracking
cooked.summary: Microsoft included a GDID tracker in Windows 11 that literally cannot be turned off no matter how many settings you toggle. It is like trying to move out of an apartment, but the landlord has secretly installed a permanent GPS tracker on your soul.
cooked.plain_english: Windows 11 contains a permanent tracking identifier that users are unable to fully disable through the operating system's settings.

cooked.headline: Managing AI agents is just babysitting code that hallucinates
cooked.summary: We are trying to build autonomous systems that can govern themselves, but we're mostly just creating more complex layers of bureaucracy for our own bugs. It turns out that when you automate the manager, you just end up with a boss that can't explain why it broke production.
cooked.plain_english: The article discusses the challenges of creating oversight and control systems for autonomous artificial intelligence agents.

cooked.headline: The ultimate plot twist: AI is so good that humans now have to prove they aren't machines
cooked.summary: We spent years teaching AI how to mimic human speech, and now we're forced to take personality tests just to prove we aren't scripts. It is the digital equivalent of a group project where you have to prove to the teacher that you actually wrote the essay yourself.
cooked.plain_english: A Chinese voice actor was required to provide extensive evidence of his human identity after AI detection software incorrectly flagged his work as computer-generated.
